this info is auto-generated so your changes will be overridden next time we generate the api using swagger. are you sure you want to marge this?

 > time we generate the api using swagger. are you sure you want to

no if it is overwritten by auto generation, this patch has no sense... :)

but then auto-generation should take the fixes into account to avoid auto generating of doc with doc8 errors. Can you give me the pointer where the processing is done
I will abandon this patch

Patch Set 1:

Hi Morgan,

This is the steps we're told to do in order to generate the APIs from our swagger and The results are as you've seen:

1. Convert from Swagger JSON to AsciiDoc

java -jar swagger2markup-cli-1.3.1.jar convert -f ./temp -i  lcm_swagger_vfc.nslcm.swagger.json

2. Convert from AsciiDoc to docbook

asciidoctor temp.adoc -o temp.dbk -b docbook

3. Convert from docbook to markdown

pandoc -s --toc -f docbook -t rst  temp.dbk > lcm_swagger_vfc.nslcm.swagger.rst


We're planning on taking the same steps for each time we want to update the APIs page from the swagger.

Because of that any manual changes will be overwritten be the new generated page.

Do you know of a better way to handle this that will allow adding changes after the page has been generated?

I am new to ONAP so I probably miss lots of context elements, sorry for that

I think swagger is better than a rst for API documentation even if I understand the need for harmonization especially for such big project


I assume many transformations could lead to doc8 issues :)

It would be better to expose swagger URL + automatic associated documentation on a web site and forget the rst, or in this rst just reference the url where such API/doc is hosted
it means that the API shall be stubbed, which is not always trivial.

In my OPNFV project, we "htmlized" and "dockerized" our API. On any new commit change we regenerate a docker and redeploy t in our production env.
http://testresults.opnfv.org/test/swagger/spec.html
http://artifacts.opnfv.org/releng/docs/testapi.html (swagger codegen)

not sure these are best practices... :)

OpenStack API guide is probably the reference https://developer.openstack.org/api-guide/quick-start/
